links.targetTitle: Live collab uses per-user op feeds plus ACT-gated collab manifest
GSOC provides many-to-one notifications with sub-second latency via symmetric shared-key SOCs.
Powerhouse documents are stored as an append-only operation log, not state snapshots

global.title: Live collaboration uses per-user op feeds plus GSOC pings to trigger debounced pulls.
global.content:
## Model

Live collaboration on a drive or document is **multi-writer**: each collaborator writes their own ops to their own feed, and everyone reads everyone else's. ACT gates read access (drive-level first, narrowed to doc-level later).

## Flow

1. Each peer mines a GSOC signer for the other's overlay (one-time, ~10–30s).
2. Signers are exchanged via PSS (trusted channel, one-to-one).
3. On op-committed, SwarmChannel pushes the op batch to the author's feed and sends a GSOC `doc-updated` ping to each other collaborator.
4. Receivers pull new batches from the author's feed via `reactor.load()`.
5. A timer-based poll remains as fallback when GSOC pings are missed.

## GSOC notification payload

```typescript
{
  type: "doc-updated",
  documentId: "b9e4fe2e-...",
  driveId: "96ffdf26-...",
  fromAddress: "0xadbA7C2F...",
  latestIndex: { document: 5, global: 12 },
  timestamp: "2026-04-15T18:30:05Z"
}
```

The payload is small (<4KB) and only tells the receiver's SwarmChannel to pull; the actual ops travel via the feed.

## Debouncing

GSOC-triggered pulls are debounced so a flurry of ops (e.g. rapid typing) coalesces into a single pull.
global.description: Each collaborator writes ops to their own feed; peers subscribe. GSOC sends doc-updated pings on op-committed, which trigger debounced reactor.load() pulls.
